# Zefer

> Client-side encryption tool. Converts text and files into password-protected .zefer files using AES-256-GCM. 100% browser-based and CLI, zero-knowledge, open source.

Zefer encrypts secrets (text, files, API keys, images, ZIPs) into `.zefer` files that can only be decrypted with the correct passphrase. All cryptographic operations happen locally via the Web Crypto API (browser) or Node.js crypto module (CLI). No data ever reaches a server.

- Live web app: https://zefer.carrillo.app
- Web source: https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer
- CLI source: https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli
- CLI on npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/zefer-cli
- Agent instructions: https://zefer.carrillo.app/agents.md
- License: MIT
- Author: [José Carrillo](https://github.com/carrilloapps)

---

## CLI (zefer-cli)

The official command-line companion. `.zefer` files are fully cross-compatible between the web app and the CLI — same binary format, same cryptographic parameters.

### Install

```bash
npm install -g zefer-cli        # recommended (requires Node.js 20+)
npx zefer-cli <command>         # without installing
```

Standalone binaries (no Node.js required):

| Platform | Download |
|---|---|
| Linux x64 | https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli/releases/latest/download/zefer-linux-x64 |
| Linux ARM64 | https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli/releases/latest/download/zefer-linux-arm64 |
| macOS Intel | https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli/releases/latest/download/zefer-macos-x64 |
| macOS Apple Silicon | https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli/releases/latest/download/zefer-macos-arm64 |
| Windows x64 | https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer-cli/releases/latest/download/zefer-win-x64.exe |

Verify integrity: `checksums.txt` is included in every release.

### Commands

#### zefer encrypt

```bash
zefer encrypt report.pdf -p mypassphrase
zefer encrypt --text "secret note" -p mypassphrase -o note.zefer
echo "secret" | zefer encrypt - -p mypassphrase -o out.zefer

# All options
zefer encrypt secret.txt \
  -p "main-passphrase" \
  -2 "second-key" --dual-key \       # two-person authorization (ZEFR3 if also --reveal)
  --reveal "reveal-passphrase" \     # recipient can decrypt without main key
  -q "Pet name?" -a "firulais" \     # secret question (PBKDF2-hashed)
  --ttl 1440 \                       # expires in 24h (minutes)
  --max-attempts 3 \                 # lock after 3 failed attempts
  --allowed-ips "10.0.0.1,::1" \    # IPv4/IPv6 allowlist (stored encrypted)
  --hint "two parts needed" \        # visible without passphrase
  --note "For Alice only" \          # public metadata
  -c gzip \                          # compress before encrypting
  -i 1000000 \                       # PBKDF2 iterations (0 = auto-benchmark)
  -o output.zefer \
  --verbose
```

#### zefer decrypt

```bash
zefer decrypt report.pdf.zefer -p mypassphrase
zefer decrypt note.zefer -p mypassphrase          # text mode → stdout
zefer decrypt file.zefer -p pass -o out.txt --force
zefer decrypt file.zefer -p "main" -2 "second"    # dual-key
zefer decrypt file.zefer -p "reveal-key" -a "answer"  # reveal key + question
```

#### zefer keygen

```bash
zefer keygen                          # 64-char base64url (recommended)
zefer keygen --mode alpha --length 32 # printable ASCII + symbols
zefer keygen --mode hex   --length 32 # hex token
zefer keygen --mode uuid              # UUID v4
zefer keygen --mode unicode --length 24 # Latin/Cyrillic/CJK/Emoji
zefer keygen --count 5 --length 64   # 5 keys at once
```

#### zefer info

```bash
zefer info secret.zefer
# Shows: format (ZEFB3/ZEFR3), mode (text/file), iterations, compression, hint, note
# Security details (expiry, IPs, question, max attempts) are inside the encrypted payload
```

### CLI flags reference

```
encrypt [input] [options]
  input                 File path, - for stdin, or use --text
  -o, --output          Output path (default: <input>.zefer)
  -p, --passphrase      Passphrase (prompted if omitted)
  -2, --second          Second passphrase (dual-key mode)
  -r, --reveal          Reveal key (creates ZEFR3 dual-block file)
  -t, --text            Encrypt text directly
  --hint                Public hint (visible without passphrase)
  --note                Public note (visible without passphrase)
  -q, --question        Secret question
  -a, --answer          Secret question answer (prompted if -q but no -a)
  --ttl <minutes>       Expiration in minutes (0 = never)
  -i, --iterations      PBKDF2 iterations (0 = auto-benchmark device)
  -c, --compression     none | gzip | deflate | deflate-raw (default: none)
  --max-attempts <n>    Max decryption attempts (0 = unlimited)
  --allowed-ips <ips>   Comma-separated IPv4/IPv6 allowlist
  --dual-key            Enable dual-key mode (requires --second)
  --verbose             Show security options before encrypting

decrypt <input> [options]
  -o, --output          Output path (default: stdout for text, original name for file)
  -p, --passphrase      Passphrase (prompted if omitted)
  -2, --second          Second passphrase (dual-key mode)
  -a, --answer          Secret question answer (prompted if needed)
  --force               Overwrite existing output file

keygen [options]
  -m, --mode            alpha | hex | uuid | secure | unicode (default: secure)
  -l, --length          Length in characters (default: 64)
  -n, --count           Number of keys to generate (default: 1)

info <input>            Show public header without decrypting
```

### Automation examples

```bash
# Encrypt all .env files
for f in *.env; do
  zefer encrypt "$f" -p "$ZEFER_PASS" -o "encrypted/$f.zefer"
done

# Decrypt and pipe
zefer decrypt secrets.zefer -p "$ZEFER_PASS" | jq '.api_key'

# CI/CD: encrypt before commit, decrypt in deployment
zefer encrypt .env.production -p "$CI_KEY" --ttl 10080 -o .env.production.zefer
zefer decrypt .env.production.zefer -p "$DEPLOY_KEY" -o .env --force

# Generate and immediately use a key
PASS=$(zefer keygen --mode secure --length 64)
zefer encrypt secret.txt -p "$PASS" -o secret.zefer
```

### CLI npm scripts (for contributors)

```
npm run build                    # ESM bundle for npm
npm run build:cjs                # Bundle for standalone binaries
npm run build:binary             # All platform binaries (requires Bun)
npm run build:binary:linux-x64   # Linux x64 only
npm run build:binary:linux-arm64 # Linux ARM64 only
npm run build:binary:macos-x64   # macOS Intel only
npm run build:binary:macos-arm64 # macOS Apple Silicon only
npm run build:binary:win-x64     # Windows x64 only
npm run build:binary:current     # Current machine only
npm run clean                    # Remove dist/ and binaries/
npm run test                     # 26 smoke tests
npm run verify                   # typecheck + build + version check
npm run release:patch            # Bump version + push tag
npm run release:gh               # Create GitHub release (triggers binary builds)
```

---

## Web App

### Encryption flow

1. User enters text or selects a file (click or drag-and-drop)
2. Sets a passphrase (minimum 6 characters)
3. PBKDF2-SHA256 derives a 256-bit key from passphrase + 32-byte random salt
4. AES-256-GCM encrypts the payload with a 12-byte random IV
5. Files over 16MB are split into chunks (unique IV per chunk via base_iv XOR chunk_index)
6. Result is packaged as ZEFB3 (single key) or ZEFR3 (with reveal key) binary file

### Web URL Parameters

Pre-configure encrypt/decrypt forms via URL. Every param has a long name and short alias. Sensitive params (`p`, `p2`, `r`, `a`) are auto-cleared from the URL after reading.

**Tab**: `tab`/`t` = `encrypt` | `decrypt`

**Encrypt params**:

| Long | Short | Type | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| passphrase | p | string | min 6 chars |
| passphrase2 | p2 | string | enables dual key |
| dual | d | flag | 1/true |
| reveal | r | string | min 6 chars |
| mode | m | enum | text/file |
| ttl | — | number | 0=never, 30, 60, 1440, 10080, 20160 (minutes) |
| security | s | enum | standard/high/maximum |
| iterations | i | number | 300000–1000000 |
| compression | c | enum | none/gzip/deflate |
| hint | h | string | public hint |
| note | n | string | public note |
| question | q | string | security question |
| answer | a | string | question answer |
| attempts | att | number | 0/3/5/10 |
| ips | — | string | comma-separated IPv4/IPv6 |

**Decrypt params**: `passphrase/p`, `passphrase2/p2`, `dual/d`, `answer/a`

**Examples**:
```
/?t=decrypt&p=myKey123
/?t=encrypt&m=file&ttl=30&c=gzip&s=high
/?t=encrypt&p=key1&p2=key2&q=Color%3F&a=blue&ips=10.0.0.1,192.168.1.5&att=5
```

---

## Binary File Format (web ↔ CLI compatible)

### ZEFB3 (single key)

```
ZEFB3 (5 bytes: 0x5A 0x45 0x46 0x42 0x33)
header_length (4 bytes, big-endian uint32)
header_json {"iterations":N,"compression":"none|gzip|deflate","hint":null,"note":null,"mode":"text|file"}
salt (32 bytes, random) + base_iv (12 bytes, random)
chunks: [4-byte length (big-endian)][AES-GCM ciphertext + 16-byte GCM auth tag] ...
```

### ZEFR3 (with reveal key)

```
ZEFR3 (5 bytes: 0x5A 0x45 0x46 0x52 0x33)
header_length (4 bytes, big-endian)
header_json (same fields as ZEFB3)
main_block_size (4 bytes, big-endian)
main_block:   salt(32) + base_iv(12) + chunks  ← encrypted with main passphrase
reveal_block: salt(32) + base_iv(12) + chunks  ← encrypted with reveal key
```

### Encrypted metadata (inside AES-256-GCM payload)

Format: `[4-byte meta_length (big-endian)][meta JSON bytes][content bytes]`

```json
{
  "v": 3,
  "fileName": "report.pdf",
  "fileType": "application/pdf",
  "fileSize": 102400,
  "expiresAt": 0,
  "createdAt": 1713484800000,
  "answerHash": null,
  "allowedIps": [],
  "question": null,
  "maxAttempts": 0
}
```

### Chunk IV derivation

```
chunkIv = baseIv with last 4 bytes XOR'd with chunk_index (unsigned 32-bit: >>> 0)
```

### Dual key combination

```
combinedKey = passphrase1 + "\x00ZEFER_DUAL\x00" + passphrase2
```

---

## Security Features

All security metadata lives inside the encrypted payload (invisible without the key):

| Feature | Web | CLI flag | Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| Reveal key | ✓ | `--reveal` | ZEFR3 format, independent salt/IV/key |
| Dual passphrase | ✓ | `--dual-key -2` | Combined with `\x00ZEFER_DUAL\x00` |
| Secret question | ✓ | `-q -a` | PBKDF2-SHA256, 100k iterations, deterministic salt |
| IP restriction | ✓ | `--allowed-ips` | IPv4/IPv6 allowlist, comma-separated |
| Expiration | ✓ | `--ttl` | UTC timestamp in ms, 30min to 2 weeks or never |
| Max attempts | ✓ | `--max-attempts` | Browser: localStorage. CLI: `~/.zefer/attempts.json` |
| Compression | ✓ | `-c` | Gzip/Deflate before encryption |
| PBKDF2 iterations | ✓ | `-i` | 300k/600k/1M. CLI auto-benchmarks if 0 |

---

## Cryptographic Primitives

| Primitive | Algorithm | Parameters |
|---|---|---|
| Symmetric encryption | AES-256-GCM | 256-bit key, 96-bit IV, 128-bit auth tag |
| Key derivation | PBKDF2-SHA256 | 300k–1M iterations, 256-bit random salt |
| Answer hashing | PBKDF2-SHA256 | 100,000 iterations, deterministic salt |
| Random generation | OS-level CSPRNG | `crypto.getRandomValues` (browser), `crypto.randomBytes` (CLI) |

---

## Tech Stack

**Web app**: Next.js 16.2.3 (React 19), TypeScript 6, Tailwind CSS v4, Web Crypto API, CompressionStream API, Vitest (125 tests, 100% line coverage), i18n: Spanish/English/Portuguese (~600 translation keys)

**CLI**: Node.js 20+, Ink 5 (React 18 for terminal), Commander 12, TypeScript 5, tsup, Bun compile (standalone binaries)

---

## What Zefer Does NOT Do

- Does not store data on servers
- Does not use cookies, trackers, or analytics
- Does not collect personal information
- Does not have user accounts
- Does not transmit passphrases or keys
- Cannot recover lost passphrases

---
